LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS SAFEWAY, INCORPORATED, AND ERIC F. ANDERSON, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES BY SAFEWAY INCORPORATED. SAFEWAYS MOTION ARE DENIED BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFFS ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS WHETHER THE ACCIDENT WAS CAUSED BY SAFEWAY'S NEGLIGENCE IN CREATING AND LEAVING A TOO NARROW PASSAGE THAT WAS INSUFFICIENTLY LIT AND WITHOUT SUFFICIENT INDICATION OF A HAZARDOUS EDGE. ANDERSON'S MOTION IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE THAT ANDERSON CREATED THE CONDITION CAUSING THE ACCIDENT. OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANTS SAFEWAY, INCORPORATED AND ERIC F. ANDERSON, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES BY SAFEWAY INCORPORATED - SAFEWAYS MOTION ARE DENIED BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFFS ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS WHETHER THE ACCIDENT WAS CAUSED BY SAFEWAY'S NEGLIGENCE IN CREATING AND LEAVING A TOO NARROW PASSAGE THAT WAS INSUFFICIENT LIT AND WITHOUT SUFFICIENT INDICATION OF A HAZARDOUS EDGE.

ANDERSON'S MOTION IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE THAT ANDERSON CREATED THE CONDITION CAUSING THE ACCIDENT. OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/PM
